# Generated by ollama (qwen3-coder:30b)
# Prompt: Write a Deno Deploy application that dynamically loads modules based on user configuration. Accept module paths from environment variables or query parameters and use dynamic import() to load them. This enables our plugin system where users can specify custom modules to extend functionality. The app runs on Deno Deploy edge runtime.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { serve } from "https://deno.land/std@0.180.0/http/server.ts";

interface ModuleConfig {
  path: string;
  name: string;
}

async function loadModule(config: ModuleConfig) {
  try {
    const module = await import(config.path);
    return module;
  } catch (error) {
    console.error(`Failed to load module from ${config.path}:`, error);
    throw new Error(`Module load failed: ${config.path}`);
  }
}

async function handleRequest(req: Request): Promise<Response> {
  const url = new URL(req.url);
  const modulePath = url.searchParams.get("module") || 
                    Deno.env.get("MODULE_PATH") ||
                    "./default-module.ts";
  
  if (!modulePath) {
    return new Response("Module path required", { status: 400 });
  }

  try {
    const moduleConfig: ModuleConfig = {
      path: modulePath,
      name: modulePath.split("/").pop()?.split(".")[0] || "unknown"
    };

    const loadedModule = await loadModule(moduleConfig);
    
    if (typeof loadedModule.default === "function") {
      const result = await loadedModule.default();
      return new Response(JSON.stringify(result), {
        headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" }
      });
    } else {
      return new Response(JSON.stringify(loadedModule), {
        headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" }
      });
    }
  } catch (error) {
    return new Response(JSON.stringify({ error: error.message }), {
      status: 500,
      headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" }
    });
  }
}

serve(handleRequest);
